# Saturday — 6-Layer Security Validation Pipeline
# =================================================
# Every line of code generated by Saturday passes through this pipeline.
# Zero tolerance for known vulnerability patterns.

## Layer 1: OWASP Top 10 Detection

### A01 — Broken Access Control
- [ ] Missing authentication on sensitive endpoints
- [ ] Missing authorization checks (role-based)
- [ ] Direct object references without ownership validation
- [ ] CORS misconfiguration
- [ ] Path traversal in file operations

### A02 — Cryptographic Failures
- [ ] Hardcoded secrets, API keys, or passwords
- [ ] Weak hashing (MD5, SHA1 for passwords)
- [ ] Missing encryption at rest
- [ ] HTTP instead of HTTPS
- [ ] Weak random number generation

### A03 — Injection
- [ ] SQL injection (string concatenation in queries)
- [ ] Command injection (os.system, subprocess with shell=True)
- [ ] XSS (unescaped user input in HTML)
- [ ] LDAP injection
- [ ] Expression Language injection

### A04 — Insecure Design
- [ ] Missing rate limiting on auth endpoints
- [ ] No CSRF protection on state-changing operations
- [ ] Unrestricted file upload
- [ ] Missing input validation
- [ ] Business logic flaws

### A05 — Security Misconfiguration
- [ ] Debug mode enabled in production
- [ ] Default credentials
- [ ] Verbose error messages exposing internals
- [ ] Missing security headers
- [ ] Unnecessary features enabled

### A06 — Vulnerable Components
- [ ] Deprecated standard library modules (cgi, urllib2)
- [ ] Known vulnerable package versions
- [ ] Outdated framework versions
- [ ] Unmaintained dependencies

### A07 — Authentication Failures
- [ ] Weak password requirements
- [ ] Missing MFA support
- [ ] Session fixation
- [ ] Token without expiry
- [ ] Plaintext credential storage

### A08 — Data Integrity Failures
- [ ] Deserialization of untrusted data (pickle, yaml.load)
- [ ] Missing input validation on API boundaries
- [ ] Unsigned software updates
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline without integrity checks

### A09 — Logging & Monitoring
- [ ] Missing audit logging for security events
- [ ] Sensitive data in logs (passwords, tokens, PII)
- [ ] No alerting on suspicious activity
- [ ] Insufficient log detail for forensics

### A10 — SSRF
- [ ] Unvalidated URL fetching from user input
- [ ] No allowlist for external requests
- [ ] Internal network exposure through proxy endpoints

## Layer 2: Language-Specific Rules

### Python
- `eval()` and `exec()` — code injection
- `pickle.loads()` — deserialization attack
- `yaml.load()` without SafeLoader — code execution
- `os.system()` — prefer subprocess with shell=False
- `__import__()` — dynamic import risks
- `request.args` without validation — injection
- `cursor.execute(f"...")` — SQL injection
- Bare `except:` — swallows errors silently

### JavaScript / TypeScript
- `eval()` — code injection
- `innerHTML` assignment — XSS
- `document.write()` — XSS
- `new Function()` — code injection
- `child_process.exec()` — command injection
- `require()` with user input — path traversal
- Missing `Content-Security-Policy` headers
- `JSON.parse()` without try/catch

### Java
- `Runtime.exec()` — command injection
- `ProcessBuilder` with unsanitized input
- `ObjectInputStream.readObject()` — deserialization
- SQL concatenation with `Statement` (use `PreparedStatement`)
- `System.out.println()` in production (use logger)

### Go
- `os/exec.Command` with user input
- `html/template` misuse
- Unchecked error returns
- `database/sql.Query` with string interpolation

## Layer 3: Authentication & Authorization Patterns

### Required Patterns
- All API endpoints must have explicit auth decorators/middleware
- Role-based access control (RBAC) for admin operations
- OAuth2/OIDC for third-party integrations
- JWT tokens must include: `exp`, `iss`, `aud`, `sub`
- Session cookies must have: `Secure`, `HttpOnly`, `SameSite`

### Forbidden Patterns
- `SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = False` in production
- Admin routes without authentication middleware
- Direct object reference without ownership check
- Token generation using predictable values (timestamp-only)

## Layer 4: Dependency Verification

### Auto-Check Rules
- Flag deprecated stdlib modules: `cgi`, `urllib2`, `commands`, `pipes`
- Flag known vulnerable patterns: `from cgi import`
- Verify package versions against known CVE database
- Check license compatibility for commercial use
- Flag packages with no maintenance in 12+ months

## Layer 5: Secrets Detection

### Patterns Scanned
- API keys: `(sk|pk|api[_-]?key)[_-]?[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}`
- AWS keys: `AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}`
- Private keys: `-----BEGIN (RSA|DSA|EC|OPENSSH) PRIVATE KEY-----`
- Database URLs: `(postgres|mysql|mongodb)://[^:]+:[^@]+@`
- Hardcoded passwords: `password\s*=\s*['"][^'"]+['"]`
- JWT secrets: `secret\s*=\s*['"][^'"]+['"]`

### Required Practice
- All secrets must come from environment variables
- Use secret managers (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault)
- Never commit `.env` files — must be in `.gitignore`

## Layer 6: Compliance Framework Checks

### SOC2 Type II
- [ ] Audit logging for all data operations
- [ ] Access control documentation
- [ ] Change management procedures
- [ ] Incident response capabilities

### GDPR
- [ ] Data minimization (collect only what's needed)
- [ ] Right to deletion (erasure handlers)
- [ ] Consent management
- [ ] Data portability (export in machine-readable format)
- [ ] Privacy by design

### HIPAA
- [ ] PHI encryption at rest (AES-256)
- [ ] PHI encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Access audit trails
- [ ] Minimum necessary standard
- [ ] Business Associate Agreements tracking

### PCI-DSS
- [ ] Card data tokenization
- [ ] Never store full card numbers
- [ ] Network segmentation
- [ ] Quarterly vulnerability scans
- [ ] Strong cryptography for cardholder data
